turns.text: Q3 of fiscal 2025 was another record quarter. Total cloud revenue was $6.2 billion, up 26% in constant currency. OCI revenue grew 48% year-over-year. Remaining performance obligations reached $130 billion — our highest ever — as AI contract wins continue at an extraordinary pace. We are on track to deliver cloud revenue exceeding $25 billion for fiscal year 2025 and are raising our fiscal 2026 cloud revenue target to $48 billion.

We are winning AI infrastructure deals that our competitors cannot match because of our cluster networking architecture and our ability to provision multi-tenant GPU clusters at scale with data isolation guarantees. We have signed contracts with five additional nation-state governments in the past 90 days. Our multi-cloud database partnerships with Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud are accelerating — customers can now run Oracle Database everywhere their data lives.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.
Safra, RPO at $130 billion is staggering. Can you give us a sense of what the conversion timeline looks like and whether demand is outstripping your ability to build capacity?

statements.claim: OCI revenue grew 48% year-over-year in Q3 fiscal 2025.
Oracle remaining performance obligations reached $130 billion, an all-time record.
Oracle is raising its fiscal 2026 cloud revenue target to $48 billion.
Oracle has signed contracts with five additional nation-state governments for sovereign AI infrastructure in the past 90 days.
Oracle Database is now available on Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud through multi-cloud partnerships.
